intro: Mutisiopersea subcordata, commonly known as the Mutisiopersea, is a plant species belonging to the Annonaceae family of flowering plants. Traditionally, Mutisiopersea subcordata has been used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues, due to its potential health benefits in reducing inflammation and fighting oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Mutisiopersea subcordata include annonaceous acetogenins, which have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and cytotoxic properties. The most common herbal preparations made from Mutisiopersea subcordata include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to alleviate respiratory issues and promote overall well-being.
